title: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 1 - The Return of the Bald Eagle
rawPassageText:
READING PASSAGE 1
You should spend about 20 minutes on Questions 1-13, which are based on Reading Passage 1 below.

The Return of the Bald Eagle

A

The bald eagle is one of the most recognisable birds in North America, partly because of its white head, dark body and wide wings, and partly because it has long been used as a national symbol of the United States. Its name can be misleading: the bird is not actually bald, but has pale head feathers that contrast strongly with the rest of its body. Although many people imagine it only above wild lakes and remote forests, the species is now also seen near towns, reservoirs, farms and busy rivers where fish are available. This ability to use different waterside habitats has helped its recovery, but it has also brought the bird into closer contact with people.

B

In the twentieth century, bald eagle numbers fell sharply. Some birds were shot because they were wrongly believed to threaten livestock, and nesting trees were removed as wetlands and riverbanks were developed. The most serious pressure, however, came from the pesticide DDT. The chemical moved through food chains and built up in the bodies of fish-eating birds. It did not usually kill adult eagles immediately, but it made their eggshells thinner. As a result, many eggs broke during incubation, and pairs that appeared healthy failed to raise young. By the time the problem was widely understood, the species had disappeared from many areas where it had once nested.

C

The recovery of the bald eagle was not the result of national pride alone. It depended on a mixture of law, science and patient fieldwork. Restrictions and later bans on DDT removed the most damaging chemical pressure. Legal protection reduced shooting and disturbance at nests. Biologists monitored breeding pairs, protected important nesting trees and, in some regions, moved young birds to places where the species had vanished. This reintroduction work often used a method called hacking, in which young eagles were raised in artificial platforms or towers until they were ready to fly. The birds were fed without direct human contact, so that they would not learn to associate people with food.

D

As populations increased, bald eagles returned to places where residents had not seen them for decades. Some nests are now found near housing developments, highways and public parks. This success can create new problems. A nest may attract photographers who stand too close, or landowners may want to cut a tree that has become unsafe. Wildlife agencies therefore try to balance protection with ordinary human activity. In many cases, a small buffer around an active nest is enough. The aim is not to remove people from every eagle territory, but to prevent repeated disturbance during the breeding season.

E

The species still faces risks. Eagles can be poisoned when they eat carcasses containing fragments of lead ammunition. Collisions with vehicles, power lines and wind turbines also cause deaths, especially where birds feed near roads or open fields. Climate change may alter the timing of fish runs or the availability of suitable nesting trees in some regions. However, conservationists point out that the bald eagle's recovery shows the value of removing the main cause of decline before a species is beyond rescue. The bird's comeback is therefore often used as a lesson in ecological resilience rather than as proof that conservation is easy.

Questions 1-6

Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 1?

In boxes 1-6 on your answer sheet, write

YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

1 The bald eagle is found only in remote wilderness areas.
2 Every bald eagle moves south during winter.
3 DDT caused bald eagle eggs to become more fragile.
4 The bald eagle recovered simply because it was an important national symbol.
5 Some reintroduction projects raised young eagles in artificial structures before release.
6 Wind turbines currently kill more bald eagles than vehicles do.

Questions 7-13

Complete the summary below.

Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.

Write your answers in boxes 7-13 on your answer sheet.

The recovery of the bald eagle

Affected by, and called 7....................

Answer signal, and became concentrated 8....................

Later, legal curbs and .................... on the pesticide eased the threat 9....................

Were returned, and method known as 10....................

Before they could fly, and answer signal 11....................

In carcasses, and continuing danger 12....................

Presented as an example, and answer signal 13....................
rawAnswerKey:
Question 1: The bald eagle is found only in remote wilderness areas.

Correct Answer: NO

Accepted Answers: NO

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 1 - The Return of the Bald Eagle

Exact Portion: Although many people imagine it only above wild lakes and remote forests, the species is now also seen near towns, reservoirs, farms and busy rivers where fish are available.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ NO เพราะโจทย์บอกว่า bald eagle พบเฉพาะใน remote wilderness areas เท่านั้น แต่ passage บอกตรงข้ามว่าแม้หลายคนจะนึกถึงพื้นที่ป่าไกล ๆ แต่ปัจจุบันยังพบใกล้ towns, reservoirs, farms และ busy rivers ด้วย หลักฐานคือ “the species is now also seen near towns...” ดังนั้นคำว่า only ในโจทย์ถูก passage ขัดแย้งโดยตรง จึงต้องตอบ NO ไม่ใช่ NOT GIVEN

Paraphrased Vocabulary: found only = contradicted by also seen; remote wilderness areas = wild lakes and remote forests; near people = near towns, reservoirs, farms and busy rivers; answer signal = NO

Question 2: Every bald eagle moves south during winter.

Correct Answer: NOT GIVEN

Accepted Answers: NOT GIVEN

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 1 - The Return of the Bald Eagle

Exact Portion: This ability to use different waterside habitats has helped its recovery

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ NOT GIVEN เพราะ passage พูดถึง habitat หลายแบบที่ bald eagle ใช้ได้ แต่ไม่ได้ให้ข้อมูลเรื่องการอพยพในฤดูหนาว และไม่ได้บอกว่า every bald eagle moves south หรือไม่ ข้อมูลที่เกี่ยวข้องมีแค่เรื่องการใช้ waterside habitats หลายแบบ จึงไม่มีหลักฐานพอจะตอบ YES หรือ NO ต้องตอบ NOT GIVEN

Paraphrased Vocabulary: winter movement = not stated; every bald eagle = no universal claim stated; different habitats = different waterside habitats; answer signal = NOT GIVEN

Question 3: DDT caused bald eagle eggs to become more fragile.

Correct Answer: YES

Accepted Answers: YES

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 1 - The Return of the Bald Eagle

Exact Portion: it made their eggshells thinner. As a result, many eggs broke during incubation

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ YES เพราะโจทย์บอกว่า DDT ทำให้ไข่เปราะขึ้น ซึ่งตรงกับ passage ที่บอกว่า DDT made their eggshells thinner และผลคือ eggs broke during incubation คำว่า more fragile ในโจทย์เป็นการ paraphrase จาก thinner eggshells และ eggs broke ดังนั้น passage ยืนยันความหมายเดียวกับโจทย์อย่างชัดเจน จึงตอบ YES

Paraphrased Vocabulary: caused = made; eggs become more fragile = eggshells thinner / eggs broke; DDT = pesticide DDT; answer signal = YES

Question 4: The bald eagle recovered simply because it was an important national symbol.

Correct Answer: NO

Accepted Answers: NO

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 1 - The Return of the Bald Eagle

Exact Portion: The recovery of the bald eagle was not the result of national pride alone. It depended on a mixture of law, science and patient fieldwork.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ NO เพราะโจทย์บอกว่า recovery เกิดขึ้น simply because เป็น national symbol แต่ passage ปฏิเสธชัดว่า was not the result of national pride alone และอธิบายว่าต้องพึ่ง law, science และ fieldwork ด้วย คำว่า simply because/alone เป็นจุดสำคัญที่ทำให้โจทย์ขัดกับ passage จึงตอบ NO

Paraphrased Vocabulary: national symbol = national pride; simply because = alone; recovered = recovery; law, science and fieldwork = real causes of recovery; answer signal = NO

Question 5: Some reintroduction projects raised young eagles in artificial structures before release.

Correct Answer: YES

Accepted Answers: YES

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 1 - The Return of the Bald Eagle

Exact Portion: young eagles were raised in artificial platforms or towers until they were ready to fly.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ YES เพราะโจทย์กล่าวถึง reintroduction projects ที่เลี้ยง young eagles ใน artificial structures ก่อนปล่อย ซึ่งตรงกับ passage ว่า young eagles were raised in artificial platforms or towers until they were ready to fly คำว่า artificial structures ในโจทย์เทียบได้กับ artificial platforms or towers ใน passage จึงเป็นข้อมูลเดียวกัน

Paraphrased Vocabulary: reintroduction projects = reintroduction work; artificial structures = artificial platforms or towers; before release = until they were ready to fly; answer signal = YES

Question 6: Wind turbines currently kill more bald eagles than vehicles do.

Correct Answer: NOT GIVEN

Accepted Answers: NOT GIVEN

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 1 - The Return of the Bald Eagle

Exact Portion: Collisions with vehicles, power lines and wind turbines also cause deaths

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ NOT GIVEN เพราะ passage บอกว่า vehicles, power lines และ wind turbines ทำให้ bald eagles ตายได้ แต่ไม่ได้เปรียบเทียบว่า wind turbines kill more than vehicles หรือให้ตัวเลขลำดับความรุนแรงใด ๆ โจทย์ถามการเปรียบเทียบมากกว่า/น้อยกว่า ซึ่ง passage ไม่ได้ระบุ จึงต้องตอบ NOT GIVEN

Paraphrased Vocabulary: kill = cause deaths; vehicles = vehicles; wind turbines = wind turbines; more than = no comparison stated; answer signal = NOT GIVEN

Question 7: Affected by, and called ....................
Correct Answer: DDT

Accepted Answers: DDT

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 1 - The Return of the Bald Eagle

Exact Portion: The most serious pressure, however, came from the pesticide DDT.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ DDT เพราะ summary ใช้ถ้อยคำถอดความ ไม่ได้คัดลอกวลีจาก passage ตรงช่องว่าง — summary ถามชื่อ chemical ที่เป็นหนึ่งในสาเหตุของการลดลง และ passage ระบุชัดว่า pressure ที่สำคัญมาจาก pesticide DDT คำที่ต้องเติมคือ DDT เป็นคำเดียวจาก passage เข้ากับประโยค “a chemical called ___” และไม่ควรเปลี่ยนเป็น pesticide เพราะโจทย์ถามชื่อ chemical


Paraphrased Vocabulary: affected by and called = the most serious pressure however came from the pesticide ddt; answer signal = DDT

Question 8: Answer signal, and became concentrated ....................
Correct Answer: eggshells

Accepted Answers: eggshells

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 1 - The Return of the Bald Eagle

Exact Portion: The chemical moved through food chains and built up in the bodies of fish-eating birds. It did not usually kill adult eagles immediately, but it made their eggshells thinner.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ eggshells เพราะ summary ใช้ถ้อยคำถอดความ ไม่ได้คัดลอกวลีจาก passage ตรงช่องว่าง — summary บอกว่า substance became concentrated in fish-eating birds and made their ___ thinner ซึ่งตรงกับ passage ว่า chemical built up in the bodies of fish-eating birds และ made their eggshells thinner คำนี้เป็นคำจาก passage โดยตรงและเป็น ONE WORD ONLY จึงเติม eggshells


Paraphrased Vocabulary: answer signal and became concentrated = the chemical moved through food chains and built up in; answer signal = eggshells

Question 9: Later, legal curbs and .................... on the pesticide eased the threat.
Correct Answer: bans

Accepted Answers: bans

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 1 - The Return of the Bald Eagle

Exact Portion: Restrictions and later bans on DDT removed the most damaging chemical pressure.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ bans เพราะ summary ใช้ถ้อยคำถอดความ ไม่ได้คัดลอกวลีจาก passage ตรงช่องว่าง — summary ถามว่า restrictions and ___ on the chemical reduced this pressure และ passage ใช้วลีตรง ๆ ว่า Restrictions and later bans on DDT removed the most damaging chemical pressure คำที่เติมต้องเป็น bans เพราะทำงานคู่กับ restrictions and และเป็นคำเดียวจาก passage


Paraphrased Vocabulary: legal curbs = restrictions; pesticide = the chemical; eased the threat = reduced this pressure; answer signal = bans

Question 10: Were returned, and method known as ....................
Correct Answer: hacking

Accepted Answers: hacking

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 1 - The Return of the Bald Eagle

Exact Portion: This reintroduction work often used a method called hacking

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ hacking เพราะ summary ใช้ถ้อยคำถอดความ ไม่ได้คัดลอกวลีจาก passage ตรงช่องว่าง — summary ถามชื่อ method ที่ใช้ในการ return young eagles และ passage ระบุว่า reintroduction work often used a method called hacking คำว่า known as ในโจทย์เทียบกับ called ใน passage ดังนั้นคำตอบต้องเป็น hacking ซึ่งเป็นคำเดียวและเป็นชื่อวิธีโดยตรง


Paraphrased Vocabulary: returned and method known as = this reintroduction work often used a method called hacking; answer signal = hacking

Question 11: Before they could fly, and answer signal ....................
Correct Answer: platforms

Accepted Answers: platforms

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 1 - The Return of the Bald Eagle

Exact Portion: young eagles were raised in artificial platforms or towers until they were ready to fly.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ platforms เพราะ summary ใช้ถ้อยคำถอดความ ไม่ได้คัดลอกวลีจาก passage ตรงช่องว่าง — summary ระบุ artificial ___ or towers และ passage ใช้วลี “artificial platforms or towers” ตรงตัว คำที่ขาดคือ platforms ซึ่งเป็นคำนามพหูพจน์หนึ่งคำจาก passage และเข้ากับโครงสร้างคู่กับ towers อย่างถูกต้อง


Paraphrased Vocabulary: could fly and answer signal = young eagles were raised in artificial platforms or towers until; answer signal = platforms

Question 12: In carcasses, and continuing danger ....................
Correct Answer: lead

Accepted Answers: lead

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 1 - The Return of the Bald Eagle

Exact Portion: Eagles can be poisoned when they eat carcasses containing fragments of lead ammunition.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ lead เพราะ summary ใช้ถ้อยคำถอดความ ไม่ได้คัดลอกวลีจาก passage ตรงช่องว่าง — summary ถาม continuing danger คือ poisoning from ___ in carcasses และ passage บอกว่า eagles can be poisoned when they eat carcasses containing fragments of lead ammunition คำที่ต้องเติมคือ lead เพราะเป็นสารที่อยู่ใน carcasses และเป็นคำเดียวตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY


Paraphrased Vocabulary: in carcasses and continuing danger = eagles can be poisoned when they eat carcasses containing fragments; answer signal = lead

Question 13: Presented as an example, and answer signal ....................
Correct Answer: resilience

Accepted Answers: resilience

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading April 2026 Passage 1 - The Return of the Bald Eagle

Exact Portion: The bird's comeback is therefore often used as a lesson in ecological resilience rather than as proof that conservation is easy.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ resilience เพราะ summary ใช้ถ้อยคำถอดความ ไม่ได้คัดลอกวลีจาก passage ตรงช่องว่าง — summary บอกว่า species is often presented as an example of ecological ___ และ passage ระบุว่า comeback is often used as a lesson in ecological resilience คำว่า example ใน summary เทียบกับ lesson in passage ได้ และคำตอบต้องเป็น resilience ซึ่งเป็นคำเดียวจาก passage


Paraphrased Vocabulary: an example and answer signal = the bird s comeback is therefore often used as a; answer signal = resilience
